action: The production .evolve/ledger.jsonl has genuine pre-hardening chain damage at line 1740 (0-indexed; 2026-05-26, cycle-107 era — the same line TestIter_RealLedger memorializes for the string-cycle unmarshal bug): entry seq=1740 chains from prev_hash 4f288f60… which matches NOTHING in the file — its predecessor's bytes were rewritten post-hoc (line 1739 sha=34a6e0ed…, its prev=cfb22b30…). Plain `evolve ledger verify` had been RED on the real ledger UNNOTICED until the L3.3 acceptance run surfaced it (it was also red on two benign seam classes — re-genesis line 15 and pre-CA.1 fork siblings 263/264/273 — which L3.3's walkChain now accepts with tight signatures; the 1740 class correctly stays broken). Operator decision needed: (a) accept permanent broken@1740 verdict and gate only on the suffix, (b) build `evolve ledger rebaseline` — an operator-attested epoch anchor entry that records the damaged region's hashes and restarts strict verification after it (similar shape to the L3.3 segment_seal anchor), or (c) seal the damaged epoch into a segment and extend VerifyDeep with an attested-epoch concept. Until then, any wiring of guards.Chain / preflight to plain verify will fail on the real host. Acceptance for (b)/(c): `evolve ledger verify --deep` green on the real ledger with the epoch anchor present, tamper after the anchor still detected.
evidence_pointer: go/internal/adapters/ledger/walkchain_seams_test.go (seam pins); go/internal/adapters/ledger/seal_test.go TestSeal_RealLedgerCopy (verdict-preservation acceptance); .evolve/ledger.jsonl lines 1738-1742; PR #81 L3.3 commit dacfe0ff
